breakClass: DateTime arithmetic helper inside an unmaterialized query: DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays/AddMinutes (plain CLR helpers in Helpers/SendMailShim.cs, NOT registered EF functions) applied to a COLUMN expression inside a server-side LINQ-to-Entities projection or Where. EF6 used DbFunctions.AddDays (canonical -> SQL DATEADD); the port swapped in DbFunctionsCompat.* which EF Core 10 cannot translate, so the query throws 'could not be translated' -> HTTP 500. Fix = EF-native (col).Value.AddDays(n ?? 0) which DOES translate to DATEADD.
why: Inside serverQuery select new; materialized at serverQuery.Count() (L3543) and serverQuery.ToList() (L3549), no boundary before projection. CLR helper untranslatable -> 500. Main quotation list (~27K rows). Legacy DbFunctions.AddDays. Port regression.
finalFix:
In Controllers/QuotationController.cs, replace line 3520:

    validity = (DateTime.Now <= DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays(b.QuotDate, (b.QuotValidity == null) ? 0 : b.QuotValidity + 1)) ? "Active" : "Expired",

with the EF-native form (QuotDate is non-nullable DateTime, so NO .Value; QuotValidity is int?, coalesce to 0). AddDays takes a double so the int promotes implicitly:

    validity = (b.QuotDate.AddDays((b.QuotValidity == null) ? 0 : (b.QuotValidity.Value + 1)) >= DateTime.Now) ? "Active" : "Expired",

This translates to SQL DATEADD(day, ...) exactly like legacy EF6 DbFunctions.AddDays, preserving the Active/Expired output (DateTime.Now <= cutoff  ==  cutoff >= DateTime.Now). Note: the same DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays-in-projection pattern exists in the ported MyReports/PurchaseQuotation/PurchaseOrder/DeliverynoteController quotation/validity grids (legacy lines listed) and should get the identical treatment per their column nullability.
reasoning:
I attempted to refute but the flag holds. (a) SERVER-SIDE confirmed: at Controllers/QuotationController.cs:3460 `serverQuery` is built as `from b in db.Quotations ... join ... select new {...}` â€” a pure IQueryable. There is NO .ToList()/.AsEnumerable()/.ToArray() boundary before the `select new` at L3499-3531. The `validity` expression (L3520) lives inside that projection, and serverQuery is enumerated at serverQuery.Count() (L3543) and serverQuery.ToList() (L3549). The author even left a comment (L3480-3482) that OTHER subqueries were deliberately moved client-side because EF Core can't translate them inside the query â€” but `validity` was left inside. The fast-page branch (L3539-3547) also runs OrderBy/Skip/Take on the server. So the fragment is genuinely server-side; not after a materialization boundary, not a standalone local, not a group-aggregate.

(b) WOULD 500 confirmed: `DbFunctionsCompat.AddDays` is a plain CLR helper in Helpers/SendMailShim.cs (L70-71), NOT a registered EF [DbFunction]. EF Core 10 cannot translate an arbitrary user static method inside a server-side projection -> throws InvalidOperationException 'could not be translated' -> HTTP 500. Entity types verified in Models/Quotation.cs: QuotDate is non-nullable DateTime (L13), QuotValidity is int? (L43).

PORT REGRESSION confirmed vs legacy: _archive\legacy-mvc5\Controllers\QuotationController.cs:3865 used `DbFunctions.AddDays(b.QuotDate, ...)` â€” EF6 canonical function that emits SQL DATEADD and worked. The port swapped in the untranslatable CLR shim.

SEVERITY high: db.Quotations is the main quotation register (~27K rows); this is the default grid-load path and fires on every list load with data present â€” not edge-only.

NOTE on proposed fix: it is right in approach (use EF-native DateTime.AddDays which translates to DATEADD) but wrong in one detail â€” it writes `b.QuotDate.Value`, yet QuotDate is non-nullable DateTime, so `.Value` does not compile. Corrected fix uses `b.QuotDate.AddDays(...)` directly.
